# MapLibre Native [![codecov](https://codecov.io/github/maplibre/maplibre-native/branch/main/graph/badge.svg?token=8ZQRRY56ZA)](https://codecov.io/github/maplibre/maplibre-native) [![](https://img.shields.io/badge/Slack-%23maplibre--native-2EB67D?logo=slack)](https://slack.openstreetmap.us/) MapLibre Native is a free and open-source library for publishing maps in your apps and desktop applications on various platforms. Fast displaying of maps is possible thanks to GPU-accelerated vector tile rendering. This project originated as a fork of Mapbox GL Native, before their switch to a non-OSS license in December 2020. For more information, see: [`FORK.md`](./FORK.md).

## Getting Started ### Android Add [the latest version](https://central.sonatype.com/artifact/org.maplibre.gl/android-sdk/versions) of MapLibre Native Android as a dependency to your project. ```gradle dependencies { ... implementation 'org.maplibre.gl:android-sdk:11.11.0' ... } ``` Add a `MapView` to your layout XML file: ```xml ```

The problem:

2. OpenHarmony's C++ environment has no `<source_location>` at all
3. The current code uses `#include <source_location>` which would fail on OpenHarmony

The user wants:
1. A standards-safe fallback without extending `std`
2. Support the normal standard-library path too (when `<source_location>` exists)
3. Update relevant build manifests
4. Commit

Let me also look at the other usages in `symbol_bucket.hpp` and `bucket.hpp`.

The strategy: Since we cannot extend `std`, we need to:
- Create our own type, e.g., `mbgl::SourceLocation` (or similar), not in `std`
- When `<source_location>` is available (standard library path), we can use `std::source_location` under the hood
- When not available (OpenHarmony), provide a fallback using `__FILE__`, `__LINE__`, `__FUNCTION__`

But the call sites use `std::source_location`. The user says "keep call sites useful for diagnostics." This suggests we need to keep call sites working while not extending `std`.

cd /app && git commit -m "[core] Add standards-safe source_location fallback for toolchains without the header

OpenHarmony's (OHOS) NDK ships a libc++ without <source_location>, so the
unconditional `#include <source_location>` in symbol_instance.hpp failed to
build there. The previous workaround defined a partial `source_location`
inside namespace std, which is undefined behaviour (the standard reserves std
for the implementation).

Introduce mbgl::util::SourceLocation (src/mbgl/util/source_location.hpp):
it aliases std::source_location when <source_location> is available
(__has_include) and otherwise falls back to a small replacement mirroring its
interface. current() uses __builtin_FILE/__builtin_FUNCTION/__builtin_LINE as
default arguments so it transparently captures the caller, keeping call-site
diagnostics useful on every platform. All core call sites and the SYM_GUARD_LOC
macro now go through mbgl::util::SourceLocation instead of std::source_location.

Register the new header in CMakeLists.txt and bazel/core.bzl."

part.text:
Done. Committed as `af4810f`.

## Summary

The old code in `symbol_instance.hpp` defined a partial `source_location` **inside `namespace std`** (UB — the standard reserves `std` for the implementation) and unconditionally `#include <source_location>`, which doesn't exist in OpenHarmony's libc++.

**New header `src/mbgl/util/source_location.hpp`** defines a single mbgl-owned type, `mbgl::util::SourceLocation`:
- When `__has_include(<source_location>)` is true (the normal stdlib path), it's a plain alias for `std::source_location`.
- Otherwise (OpenHarmony/OHOS NDK), it's a small struct mirroring the interface. `current()` uses `__builtin_FILE/__builtin_FUNCTION/__builtin_LINE` as default arguments, so it transparently captures the **caller's** location — keeping call-site diagnostics (file/line/function) just as useful as the std version.

**Call sites** (`SymbolInstance`, `Bucket`, `SymbolBucket`, and the `SYM_GUARD_LOC` macro) now go through `mbgl::util::SourceLocation::current()` instead of `std::source_location`. I verified the fallback compiles and captures the correct call-site line on both clang and gcc, and confirmed the std-backed alias path works. The guards-off `SYM_GUARD_LOC` keeps `{}` to avoid trailing-comma issues in multi-arg calls like `checkIndex(..., SYM_GUARD_LOC)`.

**Build manifests** updated: `CMakeLists.txt` and `bazel/core.bzl` register the new header; a `CHANGELOG.md` entry added per project convention.
